description: Generic inference behavior is part of developer-facing contract when callers rely on inferred types.
body: Apply when modifying generic functions, overloads, conditional types, mapped types, helper builders, schema wrappers, or fluent APIs. A change can compile locally but force callers to add explicit type arguments, lose literal inference, or widen return types. Execution action: inspect representative call sites and exported tests for inferred result types. Do not replace a precise generic with any or an overly broad annotation to silence one diagnostic. Verification signals include compile-time assertion tests, downstream typecheck, and unchanged public inference at common call sites. Risk boundary: this pack covers language/toolchain contract, not framework-specific API semantics. Use when TypeScript / JavaScript work touches repository conventions, toolchain files, changed symbols, validation commands, and runtime output. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include package-local checks, focused tests, build output, smoke runs, and command logs. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves broad rewrites, generated-file drift, stale assumptions, and unvalidated behavior changes, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
